case.title: Arms & Influence Monitor — Germany
case.publication_boundary: SafeTrace distinguishes budget authority, parliamentary approval, procurement contracts, export authorisations, production, delivery and operational use. A lawful approval, contract or company benefit is not evidence of corruption or unlawful conduct.

procurement_process.title: Planung: Der Weg zu Großprojekten in der Beschaffung
procurement_process.anchor: Projects over EUR 25 million require Budget Committee approval before contract conclusion

meko.title: Neue Fregatten des Typs MEKO A-200 DEU für die Marine

events.title: Germany authorised approximately EUR 13.1 billion in arms exports during 2025

attributes.interpretation: Authorisation values do not by themselves establish completed delivery or operational use.

events.title: Initial consideration of introducing MEKO A-200 DEU
Construction activity reportedly began in Bremerhaven

attributes.company_benefit_interpretation: Commercial participation and expected revenue are not evidence of wrongdoing.

events.title: Budget Committee approved final financing for the first four frigates
events.actor: Budget Committee of the German Bundestag
events.recipient_or_beneficiary: MEKO A-200 DEU procurement project / TKMS

events.title: First ship planned for handover to the Bundeswehr

attributes.interpretation: This is a planned date, not a completed delivery.

unresolved_questions.question: Which contractual, pre-contractual or risk-bearing arrangement permitted construction activity before the final parliamentary financing approval?
unresolved_questions.basis: The Bundeswehr states that construction began in February 2026 and that final financing was approved on 8 July 2026, calling the sequence unusual because approval is normally a prerequisite for construction to begin.
unresolved_questions.safe_interpretation: The timing warrants clarification but does not establish an unlawful contract, unauthorised spending, corruption or improper influence.
